subject: [PATCH v4 04/11] mm: zswap: add range lookup for large-folio swapin

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned the change from returning -EINVAL to -EIO in zswap_load() and asked if it has any practical implications or is simply a stylistic preference.
reviews.raw_body:
Just curious, why the change from -EINVAL to -EIO here? Does it matter
in practice or is it just a matter of personal taste? :)

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that changing -EINVAL to -EIO in zswap_load() changes the semantic meaning, but does not introduce a functional difference, and is willing to let Alexandre comment further.
reviews.raw_body:
The caller only checks for != -ENOENT so no functional difference here. I viewed
it as a change in semantic, -EINVAL meant that you passed a large folio, which
wasn't supported to -EIO meaning the request is valid but zswap cannot safely
complete a PMD-sized read from per-page compressed state.

I dont want to speak for Alexandre, will let him comment on this properly :)
But above is what I thought of when I got the patch from Alex.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: The reviewer suggested that a brief explanation of the design decision behind the new zswap_range_has_entry() function and its usage in zswap_load() would be helpful, even if it's just a semantic addition.
reviews.raw_body:
A mention of the reasoning in the changelog would be nice, even if
it's only semantic.

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch adds range lookup functionality to the zswap module, allowing it to handle large folios by scanning the covered range and checking if any slots are still in zswap. If a slot is found, the read operation fails so that the caller can fall back to per-page swapin. The patch also introduces a new function `zswap_range_has_entry` for PMD swap-entry consumers to make the same range decision before attempting PMD-order swapin.
